subject: Statistics
title: Prediction Intervals
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Prediction Intervals.

hook.text: The forecast says 100. But will it be 95 or 105? Or 50 or 150? The point forecast hides the uncertainty.

buildup.text: Prediction intervals give a range: 'We're 95% confident the value will fall between 80 and 120.'

discovery.text: Intervals widen as you forecast further ahead. Tomorrow is more predictable than next year. Uncertainty grows.

twist.text: Many prediction intervals are too narrow. They assume the model is correct — and models are always wrong to some degree.

climax.text: Honest prediction intervals communicate what you don't know. They're more useful than precise-looking point forecasts.

punchline.text: A forecast without uncertainty is a guess. Show the range.

quiz.question: Why do prediction intervals widen for longer forecast horizons?
options: Uncertainty accumulates over time — predicting further ahead means more potential for error
Prediction intervals are always the same width
